an1.150:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who misrepresent a non-offense as an offense are practicing for the harm of the many, for the unhappiness of the many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.150:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much demerit, and they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.151:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who misrepresent an offense as a non-offense are practicing for the harm of the many, for the unhappiness of the many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.151:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much demerit, and they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.152-159:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who misrepresent a minor offense as a serious offense … who misrepresent a serious offense as a minor offense … who misrepresent a grave offense as a non-grave offense … who misrepresent a non-grave offense as a grave offense … who misrepresent a remediable offense as an irremediable offense … who misrepresent an irremediable offense as a remediable offense … who misrepresent an offense requiring reparation as one not requiring reparation … who misrepresent an offense not requiring reparation as one requiring reparation — those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the harm of the many, for the unhappiness of the many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.152-159:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much demerit, and they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.160:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who correctly represent a non-offense as a non-offense are practicing for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.160:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much merit, and they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.161:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who correctly represent an offense as an offense are practicing for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.161:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much merit, and they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.162-169:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who correctly represent a minor offense as a minor offense … a serious offense as a serious offense … a grave offense as a grave offense … a non-grave offense as a non-grave offense … a remediable offense as a remediable offense … an irremediable offense as an irremediable offense … an offense requiring reparation as one requiring reparation … an offense not requiring reparation as one not requiring reparation;
an1.162-169:1.2: those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.162-169:1.3: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much merit, and they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.162-169:1.5: The Chapter on Non-Offenses, the twelfth.
